name: Ruta de las Minas – running circular from Sabero (CR-6)
name_original: Ruta de las Minas – running circular desde Sabero (CR-6)
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sabero traverse a diverse landscape within the province of León, Spain. The region is characterized by the Peñacorada Massif and the scenic Esla Valley, offering varied terrain for outdoor activities. Joggers can expect a mix of mountains and valleys, providing different levels of challenge and elevation. The natural environment includes established trails suitable for both running and walking. Best jogging routes around Sabero The most popular jogging route is Ruta de las Minas – running circular from Sabero (CR-6), a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Peñacorada Massif and the Esla Viewpoint, while exploring the region's mining history. Another top favourite among local runners is The Legions' Footprint - circular trail running from Fuentes de Peñacorada, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route features varied terrain and takes approximately 56 minutes to complete. Local runners also love the Mercadillo Bridge – Puente del Mercadillo loop from Cistierna, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through valleys and varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Jogging around Sabero is defined by the Peñacorada Massif, the Esla Valley, and the Sabero Valley. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Sabero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sabero?
A: Sabero offers a selection of 5 dedicated running routes, traversing diverse landscapes within the province of León. These routes provide a mix of challenges and scenic beauty for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in the Sabero area?
A: Yes, many of the running paths around Sabero are circular, offering convenient loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas – running circular from Sabero (CR-6)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9 km loop that explores the region's mining history. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052783646/the-legions-footprint-circular-trail-running-from-fuentes-de-penacorada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Legions' Footprint - circular trail running from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.9 km moderate path with varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Sabero?
A: The running trails in Sabero feature a diverse landscape, characterized by the impressive Peñacorada Massif and the scenic Esla Valley. You can expect a mix of mountains and valleys, offering varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31910693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercadillo Bridge – Puente del Mercadillo loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;, lead through picturesque valleys.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Sabero?
A: While Sabero's routes are generally classified as moderate to difficult, many paths are accessible and can be enjoyed at a comfortable pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas (CR-6)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9 km trail that can be completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes, making it a good option for those looking for a manageable run with historical interest.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Many routes offer glimpses into the region's rich history and natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas&lt;/a&gt;, for example, delves into the area's mining heritage while providing views of the Peñacorada Massif and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Mercadillo&lt;/a&gt; (Mercadillo Bridge) on some loops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sabero?
A: Sabero's diverse landscapes make it suitable for running across multiple seasons. The region's blend of mountains and valleys offers varied experiences. For comfortable conditions, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, though specific weather conditions can vary.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Sabero offers more challenging options for experienced runners. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Espinadal – View of Fuentes de Peñacorada loop from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 11.5 km trail with significant elevation gain, provide a demanding workout amidst stunning scenery. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sabero&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km long.

Q: Are the running trails around Sabero suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' classifications aren't provided, the region offers paths of varying difficulty. Many moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055938591/ruta-de-las-minas-running-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Minas&lt;/a&gt;, are accessible and can be enjoyed by families looking for a walk or a gentle jog, allowing them to experience the local landscape and historical sites together.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Sabero?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in natural areas like Sabero are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage for any specific restrictions on individual routes or protected areas to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes in Sabero?
A: While specific parking details for each route are not always listed, Sabero is a municipality with established trails, and it's common to find parking options in or near the starting points of popular routes, especially those originating from towns or villages like Sabero or Cistierna. It's recommended to check local information upon arrival.

Q: What is the average length and duration of running routes in Sabero?
A: The running routes in Sabero vary in length and duration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052783646/the-legions-footprint-circular-trail-running-from-fuentes-de-penacorada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Legions' Footprint&lt;/a&gt; is about 6.9 km and takes around 56 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31910693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercadillo Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; is 10.4 km and typically takes about 1 hour 13 minutes. There are options ranging from approximately 6 km to over 11 km.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sabero?
A: Runners often praise Sabero for its varied terrain and scenic beauty, offering a mix of mountain and valley views. The presence of historical sites, such as those along the Ruta de las Minas, also adds an enriching dimension to the outdoor experience.
tips: Walking through a beautiful oak grove, we find magnificent views of the Peñacorada Massif.
Walking along the Ruta de las Minas we come across this beautiful viewpoint. This is the Esla viewpoint, which offers wonderful views of Aleje and Alejico and the surrounding mountains.

At this point of the route of the Mines we find the Imponderable Mine and a spring. This mine was used to extract iron, as we can see in the color of the stones in the stream. The source has a high iron content, so it is not recommended to drink from it.
